resposta:
solution: 
```class Solution:
    def reverseBits(self, n: int) -> int:
        result = 0
        for _ in range(32):
            result = (result << 1) | (n & 1)
            n >>= 1
        return result
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(32)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a simple iterative approach to reverse the bits of the input integer. It shifts the result to the left by one bit and adds the least significant bit of the input to the result in each iteration. This approach has a high efficiency due to its simplicity and constant space complexity.
